title: First Sprint SDK Lab Templates and Question Queue (Draft)
preview:
Purpose:
This is a practical, timeboxed set of micro-labs and facilitator notes for the first sprint / first-week SDK onboarding experience. Aim: get a new engineer from cloned repo -> instrumented sample app -> deployed demo in 90 minutes. Lightweight, repeatable, and focused on observable failure modes and telemetry hooks we care about in production (latency, token counts, errors).

Audience:
- New hires (engineer, ML engineer, solutions eng) during their first week.
- Customer engineers in a 1:1 guided lab.

High-level constraints (why this differs from previous drafts):
- 90 minute total path with explicit branching for JS/Python/Go.
- Includes an unglossed \"question queue\" section (common confusion points + suggested answers for facilitators).
- Emphasizes quick telemetry checkpoints so participants leave with working APM + token telemetry.
- Minimal infra footprint: local dev + optional tiny cloud sandbox.

Timebox and flow:
- 0–10m: setup & expectations (repo, API key sandbox, terminal checklist).
- 10–30m: SDK wiring: simple generation endpoint + one embed call.
- 30–50m: Hook telemetry and basic retries (expose latency histogram + token-meter).
- 50–70m: Failure-mode drills (simulate coldstart, quota error, malformed prompt).
- 70–90m: Demo + handoff: create a tiny sample app that returns structured JSON and shows cost estimate.

Prereqs (participants):
- Laptop with Node 18+ or Python 3.10+ or Go 1.20+.
- Git, Docker (optional), local editor, terminal.
- Sandbox API key (provided by facilitator) or local mock server.

Setup checklist (for facilitator):
- [ ] repo cloned and branch per-lab ready with tiny fixtures
- [ ] pre-created sandbox API keys (rotateable)
- [ ] small demo VM / Cloudflare Worker prewarmed (optional)
- [ ] telemetry dashboard template (Grafana/Datadog link) with panels for latency, token counts, success rate
- [ ] sample prompts curated (3 pragmatic prompts per language)

Lab variants (short):
- JS (fastest): install @redwood/sdk, run dev server, curl /client/generate.
- Python (data-science friendly): pip install redwood-sdk, start jupyter notebook with examples.
- Go (backend focus): go get github.com/redwood/sdk, compile sample CLI that posts prompts.

Minimal example snippets (copy-paste for participants):
- JS snippet (1-file):
  const Redwood = require('@redwood/sdk');
  async function main(){
    const client = new Redwood({ apiKey: process.env.REDWOOD_KEY });
    const out = await client.generate({ model: 'gptx-small', prompt: 'Summarize this PR in two bullets' });
    console.log(out.choices[0].text);
  }
  main();

- Python snippet (notebook cell):
  from redwood import RedwoodClient
  c = RedwoodClient(api_key=os.environ['REDWOOD_KEY'])
  r = c.generate(model='gptx-small', prompt='Explain this code change: ...')
  print(r.choices[0].text)

- Go snippet (cmd):
  client := redwood.NewClient(os.Getenv("REDWOOD_KEY"))
  res, _ := client.Generate(ctx, redwood.GenerateParams{Model: "gptx-small", Prompt: "Make a short changelog."})
  fmt.Println(res.Choices[0].Text)

Telemetry quickhooks (must be visible by 30 minutes):
- Token-meter: emit per-request token_cost and token_count to metrics backend as tags (model, route).
- Latency histogram: instrument client.request_duration_ms with route & model labels.
- Error tagging: increment counters for 4xx vs 5xx vs timeout vs validation_error.
- Sample logs: for first 2 failed requests, capture prompt + status (redact PII).

Failure-mode drills (scripts we run during lab):
- Coldstart: flip a mock flag causing 500ms extra latency and observe SLA panel.
- Quota exceed: return 429 from mock; lab shows retry-after handling and user-facing message.
- Token explosion: craft a prompt that returns huge output and measure token_cost; practice cancelation / truncation policy.

Facilitator notes / tips:
- Encourage copy-paste over retyping; keep friction minimal.
- If participants get stuck on keys, switch to local mock server and continue.
- Use the \"show me the telemetry\" moment to explain why token=cost matters for product decisions.
- Keep one helper entirely on infra (assist with telemetry/dashboard) and one on code.

Question queue (common confusions and micro-answers for facilitators):
- Q: \"Why does the response shape sometimes change?\"
  A: models vary; show the model catalog entry and mention structured output + function-calling stubs.
- Q: \"How do I control cost per route?\"
  A: explain batching, fixed-length sampling, and cacheable prefixes; point to Optimize suggestions in Console.
- Q: \"Where are KV cache/local warmup handled?\"
  A: runtime-level caching is automatic for identical prefixes; show policy config in the example route.
- Q: \"Why are tokens different between SDKs?\"
  A: tokenizer differences and byte-encoding; show a small tokenization demo.
- Q: \"Is streaming supported in local mocks?\"
  A: yes, but we skip streaming in the 90m path; surface it as follow-up exercise.

Open issues (things to tidy before turning this into a canonical lab):
- Simplify the sandbox key rotation UX (current flow still requires manual email).
- Add one-click Grafana dashboard provisioning per cohort.
- Provide a 15-minute \"cheat sheet\" PDF tailored to non-dev PMs.
- Add a serverless example that runs inside our demo tenant to show routing/fallback.

Post-lab followups (recommended):
- Small homework: add structured-output enforcement to your sample route and submit PR.
- Pair with infra teammate to add token telemetry to one internal service.

Appendix: sample troubleshooting commands
- Quick local mock: docker run -p 8080:8080 redwood/mock-server:latest
- Curl example: curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/generate -H 'Authorization: Bearer $KEY' -d '{\"model\":\"gptx-small\",\"prompt\":\"Summarize\"}'
- Token count check (python): from redwood.tokenizer import count_tokens; print(count_tokens('hello world', 'gptx-small'))

Quick change log (why this doc exists):
- 2025-02-14: initial sketch—focus on short, telemetry-backed path.
- 2025-03-02: expanded question queue and added failure drills.

If you run this lab, please paste notes back into this doc under a new section titled \"cohort-YYYYMMDD\" so we can iterate quickly.

title: hybrid-handoff-playbook-10-90-day-activation-async
preview:
Purpose:
This playbook provides a compact, asynchronous-first onboarding and handoff template for hires that will operate in a hybrid model (remote + periodic in-office). It is optimized for fast ramp and transfer of domain knowledge for contract-to-perm and cross-functional lateral moves. The document is intentionally checklist-driven so hiring managers, mentors, and new hires can run a 10-day rapid activation followed by a structured 30–90 day growth ladder with clear success signals.

Success criteria (example):
- New hire can independently complete the basic support flow / ship a small scoped change / run the weekly oncall runbook by day 30.
- Positive qualitative mentor feedback by week 6.
- Role-readiness score >= 80% on the role-competency checklist by day 90.

Quick notes on behavior and assumptions:
- Async-first: most prep, knowledge transfer, and checks are written or recorded; synchronous touchpoints are minimized and intentionally short.
- Manager is accountable for role outcomes, mentor is accountable for daily ramp and access.
- Security and tool-access must be completed in preboarding to avoid day-1 wait states.

Preboarding (to complete before day 0)
- HR: offer packet signed, benefits enrollment links sent, contractor-to-perm conversion note updated.
- IT: laptop order, MDM enrolment, baseline apps installed (Slack, Okta, VSCode, VPN), access to repo read-only.
- Security: two-step auth enabled, training link sent.
- Manager: first-week calendar template and short intro notes prepared; expectations doc with 3-month goals created and linked.
- Mentor assigned and introduced over Slack with a short 30s intro pin message.

Day 0 checklist (first 24 hours)
- ASYNC items (primary):
  - Watch: \"Welcome to Redwood Inference\" (10m recorded intro by People Ops).
  - Read: team charter, role expectations doc, 3-month impact plan (short).
  - Complete: basic security training module (auto-verified).
  - Confirm: hardware and network health with IT via ticket.
- SYNC items (optional short):
  - 30m welcome call with manager (intro + logistics).
  - 15m mentor sync (quick plan for days 1–3).

Days 1–10: Rapid activation sprint (goal: context + first small ship/assignment)
- Day 1–2:
  - Setup dev env using written guide (mentored pair once only if blocked).
  - Run smoke tests and open a ticket documenting environment steps and any blockers.
  - Short async intro post in team channel: 5 bullet background + ask for 1 resource recommendation.
- Day 3–5:
  - Complete a guided code-read / architecture walkthrough doc and record 20m follow-up Q&A (mentor leads but asynchronous comments welcome).
  - Pick a micro-ticket (<80pt) from the starter backlog and propose an approach in PR template.
- Day 6–10:
  - Ship the micro-ticket with mentor review.
  - Run through the oncall triage runbook at least once in a sandbox (checklist verification).
  - Submit a 1-page retro: what worked in preboard, blockers, and 3 proposed changes to onboarding.

30-day checklist (solidify competence)
- Deliverable: complete a scoped feature or cross-team bugfix end-to-end with documentation.
- Manager check: 1:1 focused on expectations vs reality; update 3-month plan.
- Mentor check: run mentor scorecard (see below) and log time spent.
- Security & compliance: verification of training and access logs.

60-day checklist (expand impact)
- Deliverable: own a recurring deliverable (weekly report, small integration, or ticket queue).
- Influence: present a 10-minute knowledge share in team meeting (async recording accepted).
- Career conversation: manager provides clear promotion/role growth pathways and a list of skills to prioritize.

90-day checklist (role-readiness handoff)
- Deliverable: demonstrate ability to independently plan & ship a medium-scoped change; QA pass and stakeholder sign-off.
- Role-readiness assessment: manager + mentor + peer reviewer complete role-competency checklist.
- Next steps: assign ownership of recurring tasks; schedule a 6-month growth plan follow-up.

Mentor responsibilities (recommended cadence)
- Week 0–2: daily lightweight check-ins (async notes or 15m drop-in).
- Week 3–6: alternate day check-ins, review PRs within 24h.
- Week 7–12: weekly check-ins and handoff of responsibilities.
- At week 6 & 12: submit a short mentor evaluation note with examples and blockers.

Manager responsibilities
- Prepare 3-month measurable outcomes and publish them before day 0.
- Maintain a short weekly sync (30m) at least until week 6.
- Own performance conversations and escalation to People Ops when ramp stalls.

Evaluation artifacts and signals
- Role-competency checklist (linked artifact) for reviewer inputs.
- PR quality rubric: tests, docs, rollout plan, monitoring hooks.
- Mentor scorecard: pedagogy, responsiveness, transfer completeness.
- Onboarding retro: new-hire submission consolidated into People Ops intake.

Interview rubric notes (for contractors converting to perm)
- Use a lightweight bar for conversion: current delivery, cross-team communication, reliability, and ramp velocity.
- Recommend short async evidence pack: 3 PRs, 2 peer notes, 1 mentor evaluation.

Templates (to copy into JIRA/Confluence)
- 10-day activation tasks (checklist with owners).
- PR template for first 3 tickets (include linked onboarding docs).
- Mentor evaluation form (yes/no + example evidence).
- Role-competency checklist (scoring matrix).

Common failure modes and mitigations
- Blocker: late access to critical systems -> Mitigation: enforce "zero day-1 wait" policy in preboarding and weekly audit.
- Blocker: no mentor bandwidth -> Mitigation: fallback mentor pool and rotate 2nd-opinion reviewers.
- Blocker: low cross-team context -> Mitigation: require two short stakeholder intro calls scheduled by day 7.

Small experiments to run (people-ops runbook ideas)
- Asynchronous office hours (2x/week) where mentors drop short videos answering common onboarding Qs.
- Pair-swap week: new hires pair with a different engineer for 2hrs to broaden context.
- Onboarding retro leaderboard: collect suggested changes and surface monthly to eng managers.

Open questions / TODOs (work-in-progress)
- Should the mentor scorecard be mandatory for contractor-to-perm conversions? (pro/con discussion note to be added).
- Can we automate onboarding gate checks with a single webhook from HR system into JIRA? (technical spec request).

Appendix: quick links and checkers
- Preboard checklist (Confluence): confluence:PeopleOps/Hybrid-Preboard
- Role-competency template (Confluence): confluence:PeopleOps/Role-Competency-Template
- Starter backlog label: repo:starter-backlog/onboarding

If you use this playbook: please run the 10-day activation exactly once on each new hire and submit the onboarding retro by day 15. This doc is intended to be copied into team-level playbooks and iterated; add your micro-experiments and tag People Ops for monthly synthesis.
